Chapter 01 · Product Strategy
01
🎮 Stage 1 Unlocked
Build Fast.
Bet Often.
Strategy
01
Ship Fast & Place Multiple Bets

Focus is overrated at the start. Obsessing over a single project for months creates dangerous expectations and brutal burnout when it flops. Treat your projects like a portfolio. Out of 27, only 10 made money. That is the game.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Validation
02
Solve Your Own Problems

The best product-market fit guarantee: build for your own pain. You validate demand instantly, stay motivated naturally, and never run out of feedback. You are customer zero.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Ideation
03
Replicate What Works

No ideas? Find markets where people already spend money. Inject your own twist, design signature, or playful voice into an existing solution. Done beats original.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
AI & Speed
04
Leverage AI for Maximum Speed

The gap between idea and reality has collapsed. Use AI agents to write code, design UI, and build entire apps in hours. No excuse exists anymore.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Economics
05
Digital Margins Are Unmatched

A $1.5M digital SaaS can run at 80–95% profit margin. A restaurant needs $20M revenue to net $1M profit. The math is not even close. Software wins.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Balance
06
The 50/50 Feature Split

Divide dev time equally: 50% on product features (reduce churn) and 50% on growth features (virality). Skew either way and you stagnate.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Chapter 02 · Marketing
02
🎮 Stage 2 Unlocked
Market Like
a Hacker.
Growth
07
Build Free Side Projects as Marketing

Hate marketing? Build 100% free, single-feature tools with obvious names. No signups. Let them spread virally. Place a quiet ad funneling traffic to your paid product. Marketing that feels like building.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Sales
08
Sell It Before You Make It

Cold email a precise target audience with your solution. Ask for payment upfront. If they pay — validated. If they do not — you saved months of useless building.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Virality
09
Bake Viral Features Into the Product

Build features that are inherently shareable — think real-time 3D globes of live visitors. When users post on social, they become your marketing team. Your product IS the campaign.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
SEO
10
Programmatic SEO Cross-Linking

Every new startup gets a backlink in the footer of all older sites. Domain trust transfers instantly. New projects rank on Google in days, not months.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Chapter 03 · Pricing
03
🎮 Stage 3 Unlocked
Price Like
a Pro.
Positioning
11
Painkiller, Not a Vitamin

Rewrite your copy to frame your product as a cure for pain, not a nice-to-have feature. People spend to eliminate hurt, not to gain a marginal benefit. Change the framing, multiply revenue.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Pricing
12
Too Cheap Signals Low Quality

Paradoxically, raising prices increases perceived value and can accelerate revenue. Cheap prices trigger doubt. Premium prices trigger trust. Test upward, not downward.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Model
13
One-Time vs Subscription

One-time payments are easier to sell — ideal for validating fast. Subscriptions compound but require long-term dev commitment. Pick based on readiness, not greed.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Copy
14
Landing Page Structure That Converts

Lead with a bold promise that sounds "almost too good to be true." Then dedicate every remaining inch to dismantling objections. Headline sells. Page closes.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Promos
15
Bundle, Don't Discount

On Black Friday: never cut your price. Discounts train customers to wait and cheapen your brand. Keep price fixed — bundle with courses, templates, extras. Value spikes, brand survives.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Chapter 04 · Feedback
04
🎮 Stage 4 Unlocked
Listen to
the Silence.
Feedback
16
Chase the People Who Didn't Buy

Track visitors who left without purchasing and ask them to be "brutal and blunt" about why. Raw objections reveal exactly what to fix. Non-buyers are your best consultants.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Automation
17
Automate Churn Recovery Emails

Wire webhooks to fire personalized emails when users abandon checkout or cancel. Open a dialogue instantly. These emails reveal your biggest product gaps — and sometimes recover lost customers.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Metrics
18
Track Revenue Per Visitor

Divide total revenue by unique visitors for each project. Go deep on the highest ratio. Most founders optimize for traffic. The real metric is efficiency: how much does each eyeball earn you?

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Chapter 05 · Mindset
05
🎮 Final Stage Unlocked
The Inner
Game.
Resilience
19
Embrace the Nothingness

Most of the journey is long stretches of silence — no revenue, no audience, no validation. Get comfortable with the nothingness. Luck cannot find you unless you are still standing when it arrives.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Deadlines
20
Set Strict, Short Deadlines

A year-long project creates year-sized expectations. Short deadlines force fast launches and cap emotional investment. If it flops, you shrug and ship the next one. Protect your spirit.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Health
21
Health Is the Foundation of Hustle

Toxic hustle culture burns founders out. His rules: 8–9 hrs sleep, no screens before bed, low sugar / high protein, one intense daily workout. You cannot build from the ruins of a broken body.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Deep Work
22
Constraint Breeds Creativity

Do not grind 12-hour days. Cap deep work at 4–5 hours of total disconnection — no phone, no internet. Scarcity forces your brain to be creative and precise. More hours rarely means more output.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked

⚡ Indie Hacker Legend · Levels.io Podcast

Pieter Levels @levelsio

The Indie Hacker Bible — Straight From the Source

VC Funding

Lessons Unlocked
Scroll to unlock
Chapter 01 · Idea Generation
01
🎮 Stage 1 Unlocked
Find the
Problem.
Solve Yourself
01
Solve Your Own Problems

The best ideas come from identifying problems you experience in your own daily life. You are already the customer, the tester, and the validator. Start there.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Travel Arbitrage
02
Use Travel for Arbitrage

Travel exposes you to different cultures and technologies. Spot a solution working well in one country — like an "everything app" in Asia — and bring that concept back home. Geography creates opportunity.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Subconscious
03
Let Ideas Boil in Your Subconscious

Your best ideas will naturally emerge after "boiling" in your brain for weeks or months. Force ideation by spending 7 days writing down 3 ideas each day. Volume beats waiting for lightning.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Communities
04
Mine Niche Communities

Look at subreddits and TikTok communities. Identify the specific problems people are complaining about — then build the solution they are begging for. Complaints are free market research.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Chapter 02 · Building & Technology
02
🎮 Stage 2 Unlocked
Build Ugly.
Ship Fast.
Stack
05
Use the Tools You Already Know

Do not fall for the "framework army" pressure. Pieter built massive businesses using basic PHP, vanilla HTML, jQuery, and SQLite. The best stack is the one you can ship with today.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
No-Code
06
No-Code Is Perfectly Fine

If you do not know how to code, use whatever you can to build a prototype. Nomad List originally launched as a crowdsourced Google Spreadsheet. Constraints create creativity.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Speed
07
Speed Over Perfection

Perfectionism is your biggest enemy. A beautiful, highly-designed landing page early on is a red flag — you are wasting time. Focus on launching a minimal, even ugly page within weeks.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Manual First
08
Do Things Manually Before Automating

When Pieter launched Photo AI, he manually downloaded users' photos, ran the AI himself, and emailed results. Only automate once you have proven people will pay. Prove it first, then build.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Funding
09
VC Funding Is a Trap

Avoid venture capital. It slows you down with bureaucracy, turns you into a manager rather than a builder, and forces you into stressful growth cycles. Stay a builder.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Chapter 03 · Monetization
03
🎮 Stage 3 Unlocked
Charge Day
One.
Revenue
10
Charge Money From Day One

Do not rely on free user tiers. Free users do not convert, bring in spam, and do not validate whether your product is actually needed. The ultimate validation is someone's credit card.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Margins
11
Aim for High Profit Margins

Set pricing that makes the business worthwhile for a solo founder — $30+ per user minimum. Low prices require enormous scale. High prices let you run lean, focused, and free.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Risk
12
Avoid Platform Dependency

Payment processors can randomly ban your account when they see you succeeding. Always have an abstraction layer or alternative providers ready. Never let one platform hold you hostage.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Chapter 04 · Marketing & Community
04
🎮 Stage 4 Unlocked
Build in
Public.
Audience
13
Build an Audience Organically

Instead of expensive ads, launch on Product Hunt, Hacker News, Reddit, and X. These communities reward authenticity. Earned attention beats paid attention every time.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Transparency
14
Document Your Journey

Build in public. Share your progress, earnings, and failures. Tell stories to your users and the press to build a personal brand. Your journey IS your marketing strategy.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Feedback
15
Embrace Haters and Feedback

Do not blindly ignore critics — they often point out valid flaws like bad data or broken features. Let users leave feedback through Intercom or Crisp. Haters improve your product.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Chapter 05 · Automation & Scaling
05
🎮 Stage 5 Unlocked
Scale Solo.
Stay Lean.
Automation
16
Automate to Stay Solo

Use basic automation — simple Cron jobs — to handle repetitive tasks so you do not have to hire a massive team. Stay small, stay fast. Automation is your employee of the year.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Monitoring
17
Automated Health Checks

Create simple scripts that check if your database, sign-ups, and APIs are working every minute. If something breaks, have it auto-send a Telegram alert. You fix it before customers notice.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
AI Moderation
18
Leverage AI for Moderation

Use tools like GPT-4 to read user reviews or community chats and automatically delete spam or offensive content. AI does the moderation work of a full team. Solo scales infinite.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Chapter 06 · Mindset & Lifestyle
06
🎮 Final Stage Unlocked
The Inner
Hacker.
Therapy
19
Creation Is Therapy

When feeling lost or depressed, building things or doing hard physical labor is the best way to get out of your head. Creation gives you a daily goal. The work is the medicine.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Constraints
20
Embrace Constraints

Constraints force productivity. Work on a single laptop screen, limit your tools, and put yourself in a position where you have to ship. Limitation is the mother of execution.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Minimalism
21
Live Minimally

Getting rid of excess material possessions teaches you how little you actually need to be happy. This frees you to travel, roam, and focus on meaningful work rather than maintaining stuff.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
Focus
22
Protect Your Attention

Close DMs if they overwhelm you. Mute toxic users. Hide distracting metrics. Be a scientist of your own brain — use brown noise or fast-paced techno to induce deep focus and block distractions.

🔓 Lesson Unlocked
levels.io
